tasks.title: Move animal models from quaternius characters to dedicated animals directory
tasks.description: Animal models currently mixed in with character models in public/assets/characters/quaternius need to be separated. (1) Scan public/assets/characters/quaternius/ and identify all animal model files (e.g., bear, wolf, deer, horse, chicken, cow, pig, dog, cat, rabbit, fox, etc.). (2) Create the directory public/assets/models/animals/. (3) Move all animal .glb/.gltf files and any associated textures from quaternius to public/assets/models/animals/. (4) Update all references to animal model paths throughout the codebase: search for any imports or path strings referencing these animal models in the quaternius directory and update them to the new animals directory. (5) Update any asset collection configurations that reference animal models. (6) Verify no broken references remain by searching for the old paths.

tasks.title: Catalog all quaternius character assets for NPC designer
tasks.description: Build a comprehensive catalog of all available quaternius character assets to power the NPC designer. (1) Scan public/assets/characters/quaternius/ (after animals are removed) and catalog every model file. (2) Categorize assets by type: base body models (male, female, child if available), hairstyles (per gender), outfits/clothing (per gender), accessories, props. (3) Create a JSON manifest file at shared/game-engine/quaternius-asset-manifest.ts that exports a typed catalog: { bodies: { male: string[], female: string[] }, hair: { male: string[], female: string[] }, outfits: { male: string[], female: string[] }, accessories: string[] }. (4) Include the relative asset path for each item. (5) Add human-readable display names parsed from filenames (e.g., 'Adventurer_Hair_01.glb' → 'Adventurer Hair 1').

tasks.title: Replace NPC model Visual Assets Browser with simple dropdown
tasks.description: Replace the Visual Assets Browser in CharacterConfigPanel with a simple model dropdown. (1) In CharacterConfigPanel.tsx, remove all references to AssetBrowserDialog for NPC model selection. (2) Replace with a dropdown that lists available body models from the quaternius asset manifest. (3) Group options by gender (Male Bodies, Female Bodies). (4) Show the model name in the dropdown, not just an ID. (5) When a model is selected, load and display a 3D preview of the model in the preview area. (6) Apply the same dropdown pattern for hair style selection and outfit selection.

tasks.title: Fix NPC 3D preview in CharacterConfigPanel
tasks.description: The NPC preview in the admin panel is not working at all. (1) In CharacterConfigPanel.tsx, identify the preview rendering code and diagnose why it fails. (2) Common issues: Babylon.js preview engine not initializing, incorrect model paths, missing scene setup, canvas element not properly sized. (3) Fix the preview to: create a Babylon.js scene with a ground plane and lighting, load the selected character model (.glb from quaternius), position the camera at an appropriate distance to frame the full character, add slow auto-rotation so the user can see all angles. (4) The preview should update when the user selects a different body model, hairstyle, or outfit. (5) Show the composed character (body + hair + outfit) in the preview, not just individual parts. (6) Handle loading states with a spinner and error states with a clear message.

tasks.title: Build NPC designer with body, hair, and outfit composition
tasks.description: Create a comprehensive NPC designer that composes characters from quaternius parts. (1) In CharacterConfigPanel.tsx, build an NPC designer UI with three sections: Body (dropdown of base body models by gender), Hair (dropdown of hairstyles filtered by selected gender), Outfit (dropdown of outfits/clothing filtered by selected gender). (2) Each section should have a dropdown selector showing available options from the quaternius asset manifest. (3) The 3D preview should show the composed character with all selected parts. (4) Add a skin tone color picker that tints the body mesh. (5) Add a hair color picker. (6) Add clothing color palette controls that tint the outfit mesh. (7) Allow saving NPC presets: a named combination of body+hair+outfit+colors that can be recalled later. (8) Create a 'Randomize' button that randomly selects a gender, body, hair, outfit, and colors to quickly generate varied NPCs. (9) Store the composed NPC configuration in CharacterModelConfig so it can be used by the game engine.

tasks.title: Populate missing NPC entries in settlement character lists
tasks.description: Most relevant NPCs are missing from the admin panel character configuration. (1) Identify all character roles that should have model configurations: civilian_male, civilian_female, merchant, guard, farmer, blacksmith, innkeeper, priest, teacher, doctor, child, elder, noble, beggar, sailor, soldier, etc. (2) In the CharacterConfig type and the admin panel, ensure entries exist for each of these roles. (3) For each role, assign a default quaternius model composition (body + appropriate outfit) that visually suggests the role. (4) Merchants should look different from guards, who should look different from farmers, etc. (5) Create role-specific default color palettes (e.g., guards in darker/uniform colors, merchants in finer clothes, farmers in earth tones). (6) Ensure the settlement generation code uses these role-specific models when spawning NPCs.

tasks.title: Integrate quaternius NPC models into game rendering
tasks.description: Ensure the game engine actually renders NPCs using the quaternius character assets. (1) In the 3D game's NPC rendering code (likely in BabylonGame.ts or a dedicated NPC renderer), update the model loading to use the quaternius asset manifest and CharacterModelConfig. (2) When an NPC is spawned in the game, load their composed model (body + hair + outfit) from the quaternius assets. (3) Apply the configured skin tone, hair color, and clothing color tints. (4) For NPCs without explicit model configurations, use the role-based defaults (e.g., a merchant NPC gets the merchant preset). (5) For NPCs with no role match, randomly compose an NPC from available parts based on their gender. (6) Ensure models are properly scaled, positioned on the ground plane, and animated if animations are available in the .glb files. (7) Cache loaded model templates for instancing to maintain performance with many NPCs.
